summary: Fixed bug where ML category suggestions never reached workflow state. The submit_for_processing() handler in invoices.rs ran ML categorization and the workflow engine but never assigned the invoice to a workflow queue. Added queue assignment logic after the workflow engine returns final_status: maps Approved/ReadyForPayment→Payment queue, PendingApproval→Approval queue, terminal states→no queue, default→Review queue. Uses WorkQueueRepository::get_by_type() for lookup, move_item() for queue_items insert, and inline SQL for current_queue_id update. Non-fatal if no queue exists (logs warning, continues). Also added 2 DB integration test stubs. Build and all existing tests pass.
decisions_made: Terminal states (Rejected, Voided, Paid) return early without queue assignment
Queue lookup failures are non-fatal - log warning and return response with null queue_id
Used WorkQueueRepository trait methods (get_by_type, move_item) rather than raw SQL for queue operations, following existing patterns
next_steps: Run full DB integration tests with live PostgreSQL to verify queue assignment end-to-end
Consider adding a unit test with mocked WorkQueueRepository to test the status-to-queue-type mapping logic without a database
